Swift will be in attendance for the June 11 gala in New York City, according to CBS News' Anthony Mason, who made the announcement Wednesday on CBS Mornings.
Swift ranked #2 in Forbes’ 2025 list of the highest-earning musicians - the only artist who did not tour that year to make it.

She was also the second-most-streamed artist globally on Spotify in 2025, and the most-streamed in the U.S., according to the company.
Swift joins a list of only 7 musical legends to have been inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame at age 42 or younger, according to Billboard."}]}

21 Jan 2026
Taylor Swift once again made history when it was announced she’d be inducted to the Songwriters Hall of Fame in 2026—making her the youngest woman to earn the honor at 36 years old.. Also joining the “Mastermind” singer this year is Alanis Morissette, Kenny Loggins, KISS members Gene Simmons and Paul Stanley, Christopher “Tricky” Stewart, duo Terry Britten and Graham Lyle as well as Walter Afanasieff. The 2026 Songwriters Hall of Fame induction ceremony will be held June 11 in New York City. As we know, Taylor is no stranger to making history! In 2024, the pop star took home album of the year for “Midnights”—setting the record for the most wins by a single artist.
